name: Uruguayan Medialunas
steps: Mix the flour, half the butter, sugar, and milk into a smooth dough. Rest 15 minutes.
Roll out, dot with the remaining butter, fold over, and roll again to build flaky layers, then shape into small crescents.
Bake at 375F for 15-18 minutes until golden.
steps_detailed: Mix the flour, sugar, half the butter, and milk into a smooth, slightly firm dough. Wrap and rest 20 minutes.

Roll the dough into a rectangle, dot the remaining softened butter over two-thirds of it, fold into thirds like a letter, and roll out again. Repeat this fold-and-roll once more for flaky layers, then chill 15 minutes.
Roll the dough thin, cut into triangles, and roll each one up from the wide end into a crescent shape.
Brush with the beaten egg and bake at 375F (190C) for 15-20 minutes until golden and puffed.
Brush with a little warm honey right out of the oven for the traditional glossy, faintly sweet finish.

name: Uruguayan Torta Frita
steps: Mix the flour, melted butter, a pinch of salt, and enough warm water to form a soft dough.
Flatten into small discs with a slit in the center.
Fry in the oil 2 minutes per side until puffed and golden, then dust with sugar.
steps_detailed: Mix the flour, baking powder, melted butter, a pinch of salt, and warm water gradually until a soft, slightly sticky dough forms.
Rest the dough 10 minutes, then divide into small pieces and flatten each into a thin disc about 1/4 inch thick, cutting a small slit in the center of each.
Heat the oil in a skillet over medium heat and fry the discs 2-3 minutes per side until puffed, golden, and cooked through.
Drain briefly and dust generously with sugar while still warm.

name: Uruguayan Avocado Toast with Egg
steps: Toast the bread and mash the avocado with a pinch of salt.
Fry the egg in the butter to your liking.
Spread the avocado on the toast and top with the egg.
steps_detailed: Toast the bread until golden and crisp.
Mash the avocado with the lime juice, a pinch of salt, and black pepper.
Fry the egg in the butter over medium heat to your preferred doneness - a runny yolk is traditional here.
Spread the mashed avocado generously over the toast and top with the fried egg.

name: Uruguayan Chivito al Plato

ingredients.ingredient: Beef, chuck, short ribs, boneless, separable lean and fat, trimmed to 0" fat, all grades, raw

steps: Season and quickly sear the sliced beef over high heat.
Fry the egg to your liking.
Plate the beef topped with the cheese and fried egg.

ingredients_full.ingredient: Beef, chuck, short ribs, boneless, separable lean and fat, trimmed to 0" fat, all grades, raw

steps_detailed: Season the sliced beef with salt and black pepper.
Sear it in the oil over high heat, 1-2 minutes per side, until well browned but not overcooked.
In the same pan, fry the egg to your preferred doneness.
Plate the beef, lay the cheese slices over it while still hot so they soften, and top with the fried egg.
Serve with the shredded cabbage and diced tomato alongside.

name: Uruguayan Milanesa Napolitana
steps: Bread the chicken (flour, then egg) and fry until golden and cooked through.
Top with the diced tomato and shredded mozzarella.
Broil briefly until the cheese melts.
steps_detailed: Pound the chicken to an even thickness and season with salt and pepper.
Dredge in flour, then egg, then the cornmeal, pressing to help it adhere.
Fry in the oil over medium-high heat, 3-4 minutes per side, until golden and cooked through.
Top the fried cutlet with the diced tomatoes, oregano, and shredded mozzarella.
Broil for 2-3 minutes until the cheese is melted and bubbling.

name: Uruguayan Ensalada Rusa
steps: Boil the potato, carrot, and green beans separately until tender, then cool and dice.
Toss everything with the sour cream and season with salt.
Chill before serving.
steps_detailed: Boil the potato, carrot, and green beans separately (they cook at different rates) until all are just tender, then cool completely and dice into small, even pieces.
Combine the diced vegetables with the chopped hard-boiled egg and sliced olives.
Fold in the sour cream, season well with salt, and mix gently until everything is coated.
Chill at least 30 minutes before serving cold.

name: Uruguayan Puchero

ingredients.ingredient: Beef, chuck, short ribs, boneless, separable lean and fat, trimmed to 0" fat, all grades, raw

steps: Simmer the beef in water 30 minutes until nearly tender.
Add the sweet potato and cabbage and simmer 15 minutes, then add the corn and simmer 5 more minutes.
Serve hot with the broth.

ingredients_full.ingredient: Beef, chuck, short ribs, boneless, separable lean and fat, trimmed to 0" fat, all grades, raw

steps_detailed: Combine the beef, onion, bay leaf, and 2.5 cups water in a pot. Simmer 30-35 minutes until the beef is tender.
Add the sweet potato and carrot, and simmer 15 minutes.
Add the cabbage and corn, and simmer 8-10 more minutes until all the vegetables are tender.
Remove the bay leaf and serve hot in wide bowls with the broth and vegetables together.

name: Uruguayan Asado Mixto

ingredients.ingredient: Beef, chuck, short ribs, boneless, separable lean and fat, trimmed to 0" fat, all grades, raw

steps: Season the beef and chicken generously with salt and pepper.
Grill over medium-high heat, turning occasionally, until both are well charred outside and cooked through.
Rest a few minutes, then slice and serve.

ingredients_full.ingredient: Beef, chuck, short ribs, boneless, separable lean and fat, trimmed to 0" fat, all grades, raw

steps_detailed: Season the beef and chicken thigh generously with salt, black pepper, and the minced garlic. Let sit 15 minutes at room temperature.

Brush lightly with the oil and grill over medium-high heat, turning occasionally, 12-15 minutes total for the chicken and 15-20 for the beef depending on thickness, until both are well charred outside and fully cooked through.
Rest the meats 5-8 minutes before slicing.
Serve together, traditionally with a simple green salad and bread.

name: Uruguayan Pastel de Carne
steps: Brown the beef with the onion and stir in the chopped egg.
Spread the beef in a small baking dish and top with the mashed sweet potato.
Bake at 400F for 20 minutes until lightly golden on top.
steps_detailed: Saute the onion in the oil until soft, then add the ground beef and cumin, browning fully, 6-8 minutes.
Stir the chopped egg and olives into the beef mixture and spread it in a small buttered baking dish.
Mash the boiled sweet potato with the butter until smooth, then spread evenly over the beef layer.
Bake at 400F (200C) for 20-25 minutes until the top is lightly golden.
Let rest 5 minutes before serving.

name: Uruguayan Pollo a la Portuguesa
steps: Brown the chicken thigh, then remove and set aside.
Saute the onion and bell pepper, add the tomatoes, and simmer 5 minutes.
Return the chicken to the pan and simmer covered 20 minutes until cooked through.
steps_detailed: Season the chicken and brown it in the oil over medium-high heat, 4-5 minutes per side, then remove and set aside.
In the same pan, saute the onion, bell pepper, and garlic for 5-6 minutes until softened.
Add the diced tomatoes, oregano, and chicken broth, and simmer 5 minutes.
Return the chicken to the pan, cover, and simmer over low heat 20-25 minutes until the chicken is cooked through and tender.

name: Uruguayan Guiso de Lentejas

ingredients.ingredient: Lentils, mature seeds, cooked, boiled, with salt

steps: Saute the onion and carrot until softened.
Add the lentils and diced tomatoes with a splash of water, and simmer 15 minutes.
Season and serve hot.

ingredients_full.ingredient: Lentils, mature seeds, cooked, boiled, with salt

steps_detailed: Saute the onion, carrot, and garlic in the oil for 5-6 minutes until softened.
Add the lentils, diced tomatoes, bay leaf, oregano, and 1 cup water. Bring to a simmer.
Cook 18-20 minutes, stirring occasionally, until thickened to a hearty stew consistency.
Remove the bay leaf before serving.

name: Uruguayan Choripan
steps: Season and grill or pan-fry the pork until well browned and cooked through.
Warm the bread.
Slice the meat into the bread and top with the diced tomato and a spoonful of chimichurri-style herb sauce if you have it.
steps_detailed: Season the pork generously with salt, garlic, and pepper, mimicking a fresh chorizo-style sausage flavor.
Grill or pan-fry in the oil over medium-high heat until well browned outside and cooked through, about 5-6 minutes per side depending on thickness.
Warm the bread and split it.
Slice the meat into the bread, top with the diced tomatoes, and finish with the chopped cilantro.

name: Uruguayan Empanadas
steps: Brown the beef with the onion and stir in the olives.
Fill the tortillas with the mixture, fold, and seal the edges.
Bake at 400F for 15-18 minutes until golden.
steps_detailed: Saute the onion in the oil until soft, then add the ground beef and cumin, browning fully, 6-8 minutes. Let cool.
Stir the chopped egg and olives into the cooled beef mixture.
Make a simple dough: rub the butter into the flour with a pinch of salt, then add warm water gradually until smooth. Rest 15 minutes, then roll and cut into rounds.
Fill each round with the beef mixture, fold into a half-moon, and crimp firmly to seal.
Bake at 400F (200C) for 18-22 minutes until golden brown.

name: Uruguayan Faina

ingredients.ingredient: Chickpeas (garbanzo beans, bengal gram), mature seeds, canned, drained solids
ingredients.note: blended into flour consistency, or use chickpea flour

steps: If using canned chickpeas, blend them with a little water into a thick, smooth batter (chickpea flour whisked with water works even better if you have it).
Whisk in the oil and pepper, then pour into a thin layer in an oiled baking dish.
Bake at 450F for 12-15 minutes until set and golden at the edges.

ingredients_full.ingredient: Chickpeas (garbanzo beans, bengal gram), mature seeds, canned, drained solids
ingredients_full.note: blended into flour consistency, or use chickpea flour

steps_detailed: Blend the chickpeas with about 3/4 cup water until completely smooth (or, more traditionally, whisk chickpea flour with water and a little oil into a thin batter and let it rest 30 minutes before baking).
Whisk in half the oil, the pepper, and oregano.
Pour the batter into a well-oiled baking dish or oven-safe skillet in a thin, even layer, about 1/4 inch thick.
Drizzle the remaining oil over the top and bake at 450F (230C) for 12-16 minutes until set, golden at the edges, and lightly blistered on top.
Cut into wedges and serve, traditionally alongside a slice of pizza.

name: Uruguayan Postre Chaja

ingredients.note: tbsp, whipped, as a cream stand-in

steps: Layer crumbled sponge cake or cookies with the dulce de leche, whipped cream, and fruit in a glass.
Repeat the layers once more.
Chill at least 30 minutes before serving.

ingredients_full.note: tbsp, whipped with a little sugar

steps_detailed: Whip the sour cream with the sugar until it lightens slightly in texture (it won't fully whip like heavy cream, but it thickens and lightens).
In a glass or bowl, layer crumbled sponge cake or plain cookies, a spoonful of dulce de leche, a layer of the whipped cream, and a spoonful of the fruit nectar or diced fruit.
Repeat the layers once or twice more, finishing with a generous topping of dulce de leche.
Chill at least 30-45 minutes before serving so the layers meld slightly.

name: Uruguayan Alfajores
steps: Cream the butter with a little sugar, then work in the flour and cornstarch to form a soft dough.
Roll thin, cut into small rounds, and bake at 325F for 8-10 minutes until pale and just set.
Sandwich pairs together with the dulce de leche.
steps_detailed: Cream the butter and sugar together until light, then mix in the egg yolk.
Sift together the flour and cornstarch and fold into the butter mixture until a soft, slightly crumbly dough forms. Wrap and chill 20 minutes.
Roll the dough to about 1/4 inch thick and cut into small rounds.
Bake at 325F (165C) for 8-10 minutes - they should stay pale, not browned.
Let cool completely, then sandwich pairs together with a generous layer of dulce de leche.
For the classic finish, dust with a little cocoa powder or melt a small amount of chocolate to coat the edges, if you'd like.

name: Uruguayan Pasta Frola

ingredients.note: tbsp, as a filling stand-in for quince paste

steps: Cream the butter and sugar, then beat in the egg. Work in the flour to form a soft dough; chill 20 minutes.
Press two-thirds of the dough into a small tart pan, spread with the dulce de leche, and top with strips of the remaining dough in a lattice.
Bake at 350F for 25-30 minutes until golden.
steps_detailed: Cream the butter and sugar until light and fluffy, then beat in the egg and vanilla.
Sift together the flour and baking powder and work into the butter mixture until a soft, cohesive dough forms. Wrap and chill 20-30 minutes.
Press about two-thirds of the dough into the bottom and up the sides of a small tart pan.
Spread the dulce de leche evenly over the base (this stands in for the traditional membrillo/quince paste, giving a similar sweet, thick filling).
Roll the remaining dough and cut into strips, arranging them in a lattice pattern over the filling.
Bake at 350F (175C) for 25-30 minutes until the crust is golden.
Let cool before slicing, as the filling firms up as it cools.

name: Uruguayan Chimichurri
steps: Combine the cilantro, garlic, oil, and vinegar in a bowl.
Season generously with salt and a pinch of dried oregano if you have it.
Let sit at least 15 minutes before serving.
steps_detailed: Finely chop the cilantro and combine with the minced garlic and jalapeno in a bowl.
Whisk in the oil, vinegar, and oregano, and season generously with salt.
Let sit at room temperature at least 20-30 minutes before serving so the flavors develop.

name: Uruguayan Salsa Criolla
steps: Combine the tomato, onion, and bell pepper in a bowl.
Stir in the vinegar and a drizzle of oil, and season with salt.
Let sit 10-15 minutes before serving over grilled meat.
steps_detailed: Finely chop the tomato, onion, and bell pepper and combine in a bowl.
Stir in the vinegar, oil, and cilantro, and season well with salt.
Let it sit at room temperature for 15-20 minutes so the vegetables soften slightly and the flavors meld.
Spoon over grilled or fried meats.

name: Uruguayan Salsa Golf
steps: Whisk the sour cream, tomato paste, and lime juice together until smooth and pale orange.
Season with a pinch of salt.
Serve with fries or shrimp.
steps_detailed: Whisk the sour cream, tomato paste, lime juice, and Worcestershire sauce together until completely smooth and a pale orange-pink color.
Season with a small pinch of salt, tasting and adjusting the tartness with a touch more lime juice if you like.
Chill briefly before serving with fries, fried seafood, or as a dip.
